title: VIDAA Platform App Crash or Freeze
shortDescription: Apps on the VIDAA smart TV platform crash, freeze, or fail to open.
fullDescription:
Hisense TVs running the VIDAA operating system may experience app crashes where streaming apps like Netflix, YouTube, or Prime Video stop responding, close unexpectedly, or fail to launch at all.

VIDAA is Hisense's proprietary smart TV platform. App crashes are commonly caused by insufficient memory, corrupted app cache data, or outdated app versions. The VIDAA platform manages apps differently from Roku or Google TV, with apps stored locally on the TV.

Clearing the app cache or reinstalling the problematic app resolves most crashes. If multiple apps crash simultaneously, the issue is more likely with the VIDAA system itself rather than individual apps.
symptoms: TV becomes sluggish when using apps

fixSteps.title: Clear app cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > select the problematic app > Clear Cache. This removes temporary data that may be corrupted.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the app
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the app from Settings > Apps > select app > Uninstall. Then reinstall it from the VIDAA app store.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds, hold the power button for 30 seconds, then plug it back in. This clears the system memory.

fixSteps.title: Update VIDAA software
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > System Update and install any available updates. VIDAA updates often fix app compatibility issues.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Reset to restore the TV to factory defaults. This clears all apps and data, requiring setup from scratch.

whenToCallPro: VIDAA app crashes are software issues and rarely need professional repair. Contact Hisense support only if issues persist after a factory reset.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense VIDAA TV app crash fix

faq.q: What is VIDAA on Hisense TV?
faq.a: VIDAA is Hisense's proprietary smart TV operating system. It provides access to streaming apps, settings, and smart features.

faq.q: Can I install apps not in the VIDAA store?
faq.a: No, VIDAA only supports apps available in its built-in app store. For additional apps, connect an external streaming device.

title: VIDAA System Running Slowly
shortDescription: The VIDAA interface responds slowly to inputs and apps take a long time to load.
fullDescription:
A slow VIDAA system on Hisense TVs manifests as delayed responses to remote control inputs, slow app loading times, and sluggish navigation through menus. The TV may take several seconds to register button presses or switch between apps.

The primary cause is excessive cached data consuming the TV's limited internal storage and memory. Over time, as apps store more data and temporary files accumulate, the system slows down significantly.

Hisense TVs have relatively limited processing power compared to dedicated streaming devices, so keeping the system clean and minimizing the number of installed apps helps maintain performance.
symptoms: Apps take a long time to open

fixSteps.title: Clear system cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > Clear Cache or Settings > Storage > Clear Cache to remove temporary files from all apps.

fixSteps.title: Remove unused apps
fixSteps.instruction: Uninstall apps you do not use regularly. Go to Settings > Apps and remove any unnecessary applications to free up memory.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 2 minutes. This clears the RAM and stops all background processes, giving the system a fresh start.

fixSteps.title: Update system software
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > System Update. Performance improvements are often included in software updates.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, perform a factory reset from Settings > Support > Reset. This restores the TV to its original speed.

whenToCallPro: System slowness is a software issue. Consider using an external streaming device for better app performance instead of seeking repair.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV VIDAA slow fix speed up

faq.q: Why is my Hisense TV so slow?
faq.a: Hisense TVs accumulate cached data over time which slows down the system. Clear the cache and remove unused apps to improve performance.

faq.q: Will a streaming stick make my Hisense TV faster?
faq.a: A dedicated streaming device like Roku or Fire TV Stick will provide faster app performance, though the TV's own menus and HDMI switching will remain the same speed.

title: Wi-Fi Connection Failure
shortDescription: The TV cannot connect to or maintain a Wi-Fi connection.
fullDescription:
Wi-Fi connectivity issues on Hisense TVs prevent the TV from connecting to your wireless network, causing streaming apps and smart features to be unavailable. The TV may fail to find networks, fail to authenticate, or repeatedly disconnect.

Hisense TVs support both 2.4GHz and 5GHz Wi-Fi bands. Connection issues are often related to the router's configuration, signal strength, or network congestion. Some Hisense models have known issues with certain router brands.

Using a wired Ethernet connection is the most reliable workaround, but most Wi-Fi issues can be resolved through network settings adjustments.

causes.cause: Router too far away or signal blocked

symptoms: Connected to Wi-Fi but no internet

fixSteps.title: Re-enter Wi-Fi password
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > WiFi, select your network, and carefully re-enter the password. Pay attention to uppercase letters and special characters.

fixSteps.title: Restart TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the TV and router for 60 seconds. Plug in the router first, wait 2 minutes, then power on the TV.

fixSteps.title: Connect to specific band
fixSteps.instruction: If your router has separate 2.4GHz and 5GHz network names, try each one. Use 2.4GHz for better range or 5GHz for faster speeds at close range.

fixSteps.title: Set static IP and DNS
fixSteps.instruction: In the TV's network settings, switch from DHCP to manual/static. Set the IP address, gateway, and DNS (8.8.8.8) manually.

fixSteps.title: Use Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable from the router directly to the TV for a guaranteed stable connection.

whenToCallPro: If the TV cannot find any Wi-Fi networks after a factory reset and Ethernet works fine, the Wi-Fi module may be defective.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV WiFi not connecting fix

faq.q: Why won't my Hisense TV connect to Wi-Fi?
faq.a: The most common causes are an incorrect password, weak signal, or router incompatibility. Try restarting both the TV and router first.

faq.q: Does my Hisense TV have an Ethernet port?
faq.a: Most Hisense smart TVs include an Ethernet port on the back panel. Using a wired connection eliminates all Wi-Fi related issues.

title: HDMI No Signal Detected
shortDescription: The TV displays a 'No Signal' message when an HDMI source is connected.
fullDescription:
The No Signal error on Hisense TVs appears when the selected HDMI input port is not receiving a signal from a connected device. This is one of the most frequently reported issues across all Hisense TV models.

The issue is most commonly caused by selecting the wrong input, a loose cable connection, or an HDMI handshake failure. For 4K content, the HDMI input may need to be configured for enhanced mode to support the higher bandwidth required.

Hisense TVs have an HDMI format setting that must match the capabilities of the connected device for the signal to pass through correctly.

causes.cause: HDMI format not set for 4K device

symptoms: Black screen with HDMI device on
Input shows briefly then goes to No Signal
Only certain HDMI ports have the issue

fixSteps.title: Select correct input
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Input/Source button on the remote and select the HDMI port number your device is connected to.

fixSteps.title: Reseat HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the HDMI cable from both the TV and device, then reconnect firmly. Try a different HDMI port on the TV.

fixSteps.title: Set HDMI format to Enhanced
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > HDMI Format and set the port to Enhanced for 4K devices. Standard mode limits the signal to 1080p.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle all devices
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both the TV and connected device. Unplug both for 30 seconds. Turn on the source device first, wait 10 seconds, then turn on the TV.

fixSteps.title: Try a different HDMI cable
fixSteps.instruction: Use a certified Premium High Speed or Ultra High Speed HDMI cable. Cheap or damaged cables are a common cause of signal issues.

whenToCallPro: If no HDMI port works with multiple cables and devices after trying all troubleshooting steps, the HDMI board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV HDMI no signal fix

faq.q: Why does my Hisense TV say no signal?
faq.a: Check that you have selected the correct HDMI input and that the cable is firmly connected. Also verify the source device is turned on and outputting a signal.

faq.q: How do I enable 4K on Hisense TV HDMI?
faq.a: Go to Settings > Display & Sound > HDMI Format and set the port to Enhanced. This enables full 4K HDR signal pass-through.

title: HDMI ARC/eARC No Audio Output
shortDescription: No audio is sent to a soundbar or receiver through the HDMI ARC/eARC port.
fullDescription:
When HDMI ARC or eARC is not working on a Hisense TV, the soundbar or AV receiver connected to the ARC port receives no audio. The TV picture works normally but sound either comes from the TV speakers or is completely absent.

Hisense TVs typically have the ARC feature on HDMI 2 (check the label on the back of your specific model). Both CEC and the correct audio output settings must be configured for ARC to function properly.

Audio format compatibility is a common issue where the TV tries to output Dolby Digital or other formats that the soundbar cannot decode, resulting in silence.

causes.cause: CEC not enabled on TV or soundbar
Audio output format incompatible with soundbar
HDMI cable does not support ARC

symptoms: No sound from soundbar through ARC
Audio plays from TV speakers instead

fixSteps.title: Verify ARC port
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the soundbar to the HDMI port labeled ARC on the Hisense TV (usually HDMI 2). Use the soundbar's HDMI OUT or ARC port.

fixSteps.title: Enable CEC (HDMI Control)
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > HDMI & CEC and enable HDMI Control, ARC, and System Audio Control.

fixSteps.title: Set audio output
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Audio Output and select ARC. Set Digital Audio Format to PCM if the soundbar is basic, or Auto/Passthrough for surround-capable devices.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both devices
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both the TV and soundbar for 60 seconds. Plug in and turn on the soundbar first, then the TV.

fixSteps.title: Try optical cable as alternative
fixSteps.instruction: If ARC continues to fail, connect a digital optical (TOSLINK) cable between the TV and soundbar as a reliable alternative.

whenToCallPro: If the ARC port is confirmed non-functional after testing with different soundbars and cables, the HDMI board may need service.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV HDMI ARC not working fix

faq.q: Which HDMI port is ARC on Hisense TV?
faq.a: On most Hisense TVs, HDMI 2 is the ARC port. Check the labels printed next to the HDMI ports on the back of your TV.

faq.q: What is the difference between ARC and eARC?
faq.a: ARC supports basic surround sound (Dolby Digital 5.1). eARC supports higher quality formats like Dolby Atmos and DTS:X, but requires HDMI 2.1 cables.

title: No Audio Output from TV
shortDescription: The TV produces no sound through built-in speakers or any output.
fullDescription:
When a Hisense TV has no sound, the picture displays normally but audio is completely absent. This can affect the built-in speakers, HDMI ARC output, headphone jack, or all audio outputs simultaneously.

The most common causes are the TV being muted, the audio output being directed to a disconnected external device, or incorrect audio format settings. After connecting and then disconnecting a soundbar, the TV may remain set to external audio output.

If the issue affects only specific apps or channels, it is likely an audio codec compatibility problem rather than a hardware issue.

causes.cause: TV muted or volume at zero
Audio output set to external device

symptoms: Volume bar shows but no audio
Sound on some apps but not others
Audio works on HDMI but not internal speakers

fixSteps.title: Check mute and volume
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Mute button to unmute. Press Volume Up to increase volume. Verify the volume indicator appears on screen.

fixSteps.title: Set audio to TV speakers
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Audio Output and select TV Speakers. If it shows ARC or Bluetooth, change it.

fixSteps.title: Change audio format
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Digital Audio Format and set to PCM. This is the most universally compatible format.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 60 seconds, press the power button for 30 seconds, then plug back in.

fixSteps.title: Reset sound settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Reset Sound or perform a full factory reset from Settings > Support > Reset.

whenToCallPro: If sound works through headphones or external speakers but not the built-in speakers, the internal speakers or amplifier may need replacement.

faq.q: Why does my Hisense TV have picture but no sound?
faq.a: Check that the TV is not muted and that audio output is set to TV Speakers rather than an external device.

faq.q: Why does sound work on some apps but not others?
faq.a: Some apps output audio in formats the TV cannot decode. Change Digital Audio Format to PCM in the Sound settings.

title: Firmware or Software Update Failed
shortDescription: A firmware or system update could not be downloaded or installed.
fullDescription:
Update failures on Hisense TVs occur when the TV cannot download or install a firmware update. The TV may show an error message, get stuck on the update screen, or revert to the previous firmware version.

Hisense TVs receive over-the-air (OTA) updates that include bug fixes, new features, and security patches. These updates require a stable internet connection throughout the download and installation process.

If the OTA update fails repeatedly, Hisense provides USB firmware files on their support website that can be installed manually, bypassing any internet-related issues.

causes.cause: Hisense update servers busy or down

fixSteps.title: Retry the update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > System Update and try again. Wait 24 hours if servers may be busy.

fixSteps.title: Use Ethernet for update
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the TV via Ethernet cable for a more reliable download during the update process.

fixSteps.title: Clear storage space
fixSteps.instruction: Delete unused apps and clear cache from Settings > Apps to free up storage space needed for the update.

fixSteps.title: USB firmware update
fixSteps.instruction: Visit hisense.com/support, find your model, download the firmware to a FAT32 USB drive, insert into TV, and follow on-screen instructions.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset then update
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset from Settings > Support > Reset, then attempt the update immediately after initial setup.

whenToCallPro: If the TV is stuck in a boot loop after a failed update and USB recovery does not work, professional firmware recovery or main board replacement may be needed.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV firmware update failed fix

faq.q: Can I update my Hisense TV without internet?
faq.a: Yes, download the firmware from Hisense's support website to a USB drive and install it manually through the TV's USB port.

faq.q: What happens if I unplug my TV during an update?
faq.a: Unplugging during a firmware update can corrupt the software and potentially require a USB recovery or professional repair.

title: Black Screen with or without Sound
shortDescription: The TV screen is black while the TV appears to be powered on.
fullDescription:
A black screen on a Hisense TV with the power indicator lit means the TV is receiving power but not producing an image. If audio can be heard, the problem is likely with the LED backlight rather than the main processing board.

The flashlight test is the most important diagnostic step: shine a flashlight close to the screen in a dark room. If you can see a faint image, the LED backlights have failed and need replacement.

Temporary black screens caused by software glitches are common and resolve with a simple power cycle. Persistent black screens, especially with visible faint images under flashlight, indicate a hardware repair is needed.

causes.cause: Power supply not delivering correct voltage

symptoms: Black screen with power LED on
Faint image visible with flashlight test
Screen goes black shortly after powering on

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the TV
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 5 minutes while holding the power button for 30 seconds. Plug back in and try again.

fixSteps.title: Flashlight test
fixSteps.instruction: In a dark room with the TV on, hold a flashlight against the screen. If you see a faint image, the backlight has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check inputs
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Input/Source button and switch between inputs. Try the home screen to rule out a specific input issue.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset blind
fixSteps.instruction: If you can hear the TV, try navigating to Settings > Support > Reset using the remote buttons. You may need someone to read the steps while you press buttons.

fixSteps.title: Professional backlight repair
fixSteps.instruction: If the flashlight test confirms backlight failure, seek professional repair. The TV panel must be fully disassembled to replace LED strips.

whenToCallPro: If the flashlight test reveals a faint image, professional backlight repair is needed. This is the most common hardware cause of a black screen.

faq.q: Why is my Hisense TV screen black but I hear sound?
faq.a: This usually indicates a backlight failure. Perform the flashlight test: shine a light on the screen to see if a faint image is visible.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing a Hisense TV with a black screen?
faq.a: If the backlight has failed, repair costs $100-200 which is often worthwhile for larger screen sizes. A cracked panel is usually not worth repairing.

title: Screen Flickering or Flashing
shortDescription: The TV display flickers, flashes, or has inconsistent brightness.
fullDescription:
Screen flickering on Hisense TVs appears as rapid changes in brightness, visible strobing, or sections of the screen flashing. The problem can range from barely noticeable to unwatchable.

Hisense TVs with local dimming (ULED models) are more susceptible to visible flickering when the dimming zones adjust quickly between bright and dark content. The Dynamic Backlight and Local Dimming features can cause this when set too aggressively.

Flickering that appears only on specific content or inputs may be caused by refresh rate mismatches rather than hardware issues. Hardware-related flickering tends to worsen over time and appears across all inputs.

causes.cause: Local dimming or dynamic backlight settings too high
Refresh rate mismatch with source content

symptoms: Flickering only on certain inputs or apps

fixSteps.title: Adjust local dimming
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Local Dimming and set it to Low or Off. This is the most common cause of flickering on Hisense ULED TVs.

fixSteps.title: Turn off dynamic backlight
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Backlight > Dynamic Backlight and disable it. This prevents automatic brightness adjustments.

fixSteps.title: Disable motion smoothing
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Motion Enhancement and turn it off. This removes processing that can cause visual artifacts.

fixSteps.title: Change picture mode
fixSteps.instruction: Try switching to a different picture mode like Cinema, Filmmaker, or Standard. Vivid and Dynamic modes use more aggressive processing.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Support > System Update. Flickering bugs are often patched in firmware updates.

whenToCallPro: If flickering persists after disabling all picture processing features and updating firmware, the LED driver or internal connections may need professional repair.

faq.q: Why does my Hisense TV flicker in dark scenes?
faq.a: This is typically caused by the local dimming feature. Turn Local Dimming to Low or Off in the Picture settings.

faq.q: Does screen flickering mean my TV is broken?
faq.a: Not usually. Most flickering on Hisense TVs is caused by picture processing settings. Hardware-caused flickering that worsens over time may need repair.

title: Horizontal Lines on Screen
shortDescription: Horizontal lines or bands appear across the TV display.
fullDescription:
Horizontal lines on a Hisense TV screen appear as thin colored lines, wide bands of discoloration, or horizontal stripes across the display. The lines may be present across the entire screen or only in certain sections.

The most common hardware cause is a loose ribbon cable connecting the T-Con board to the display panel. Temperature changes can cause these connections to expand and contract, eventually loosening the cable. A faulty T-Con board is another frequent cause.

If lines appear only on specific inputs but not others (for example, only on HDMI but not on the home screen), the issue may be with the source device or cable rather than the TV.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable between T-Con and panel

symptoms: Horizontal lines visible across the screen
Lines appear on all inputs and apps
Lines worsen as TV heats up
Half the screen has lines, other half normal
Color bands or stripes in the image

fixSteps.title: Check all inputs
fixSteps.instruction: Switch between HDMI inputs and the TV home screen. If lines only appear on one input, the issue is with that source device or cable.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle for 30 minutes
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 30 minutes to allow all components to cool and discharge. Temperature-related cable issues may resolve after cooling.

fixSteps.title: Gentle pressure test
fixSteps.instruction: Very carefully press on the back of the TV near the top edge where ribbon cables connect. If lines disappear with gentle pressure, a cable is loose.

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cables
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel screws, locate the ribbon cables from the T-Con board to the panel, and carefully disconnect and reconnect each one.

fixSteps.title: Replace T-Con board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating cables does not fix the issue, the T-Con board may need replacement. Note the part number on the existing board when ordering.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable opening the TV, or if the panel itself is cracked or damaged, professional repair is recommended.

faq.q: Can horizontal lines on a Hisense TV be fixed?
faq.a: Yes, in many cases reseating ribbon cables or replacing the T-Con board fixes the issue. These are affordable repairs.

faq.q: Are horizontal lines covered under warranty?
faq.a: If the TV is within Hisense's warranty period (typically 1-2 years) and the lines are caused by a manufacturing defect, the repair should be covered.

title: TV Stuck on Hisense Logo Screen
shortDescription: The TV displays the Hisense logo at startup and never reaches the home screen.
fullDescription:
When a Hisense TV gets stuck on the startup logo, the operating system cannot complete its boot sequence. The TV may show the Hisense logo indefinitely, or cycle between the logo and a black screen in a boot loop.

This is most commonly caused by corrupted firmware, often resulting from a failed software update or sudden power loss during operation. Full internal storage can also prevent the system from booting properly.

A hardware factory reset using the physical reset button on the TV is usually the first successful fix. USB firmware recovery is available as a backup if the reset does not work.
symptoms: TV cycles between logo and black screen
Issue started after an update or power outage

fixSteps.title: Extended power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV for 15 minutes while holding the power button for 30 seconds. This gives the system a complete reset.

fixSteps.title: Hardware reset button
fixSteps.instruction: Find the reset pinhole on the back or bottom of the TV. Press and hold with a paperclip for 15 seconds while the TV is plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Recovery mode reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the TV. Hold the power button on the TV while plugging it back in. Continue holding for 30 seconds to enter recovery mode if available.

fixSteps.title: USB firmware recovery
fixSteps.instruction: Download the firmware for your exact model from hisense.com/support. Copy to a FAT32 formatted USB drive and follow the USB update procedure.

fixSteps.title: Contact Hisense support
fixSteps.instruction: If recovery methods fail, contact Hisense support for warranty service or advanced recovery procedures.

whenToCallPro: If the TV remains stuck after hardware reset and USB firmware recovery, the main board may be faulty and need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV stuck on logo fix

faq.q: Why is my Hisense TV stuck on the logo?
faq.a: This usually indicates corrupted firmware. Try the hardware reset button first, then USB firmware recovery if needed.

faq.q: Where is the reset button on a Hisense TV?
faq.a: The reset pinhole is usually on the back or bottom of the TV near the ports. You need a paperclip or pin to press it.

title: Remote Control Not Responding
shortDescription: The Hisense remote control does not operate the TV or responds only intermittently.
fullDescription:
Hisense TV remotes come in two types: standard infrared (IR) remotes and Bluetooth voice remotes. When the remote stops working, the issue differs based on which type you have.

IR remotes require a clear line of sight to the TV's sensor and are affected by obstacles, distance, and sunlight interference. Bluetooth remotes need to be paired and can lose their connection after battery replacement or firmware updates.

Battery issues are by far the most common cause, as Hisense remotes can drain batteries relatively quickly, especially the voice-enabled models that maintain a constant Bluetooth connection.
symptoms: Remote does not control TV at all
Only works from very close range
Some buttons work but others do not

fixSteps.title: Replace batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Insert fresh AAA batteries with correct polarity. Hisense remotes typically use 2 AAA batteries.

fixSteps.title: Reset the remote
fixSteps.instruction: Remove batteries, press and hold the power button for 20 seconds, then insert fresh batteries.

fixSteps.title: Re-pair Bluetooth remote
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the Home and Back buttons (or the pairing button) for 5-10 seconds until the LED blinks rapidly. The TV should show a pairing message.

fixSteps.title: Check IR path
fixSteps.instruction: For IR remotes, remove any objects blocking the bottom center of the TV screen where the IR sensor is located.

fixSteps.title: Use RemoteNOW app
fixSteps.instruction: Download the Hisense RemoteNOW app on your smartphone as a temporary remote while troubleshooting the physical remote.

whenToCallPro: Remote issues rarely require professional help. If the TV's IR sensor is confirmed faulty, the main board may need service.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV remote not working fix

faq.q: How do I pair a Hisense Bluetooth remote?
faq.a: Hold the Home and Back buttons together for 5-10 seconds. The remote LED will blink and the TV should show a pairing dialog.

faq.q: Can I use a universal remote with Hisense TV?
faq.a: Yes, most universal remotes support Hisense TVs. You can also use the free Hisense RemoteNOW smartphone app.

title: Excessive Streaming Buffering
shortDescription: Streaming apps buffer constantly or take a very long time to load content.
fullDescription:
Excessive buffering on Hisense TVs causes streaming content to pause frequently for loading, resulting in a poor viewing experience. The issue can affect all streaming apps or only specific ones.

Buffering is primarily caused by insufficient internet bandwidth or an unstable Wi-Fi connection. For HD streaming, a minimum of 5 Mbps is needed, while 4K content requires at least 25 Mbps. The actual throughput to the TV may be lower than your internet plan speed due to Wi-Fi distance and congestion.

The TV's processing power also plays a role. Older or entry-level Hisense models may struggle with 4K content from demanding apps, leading to buffering even with adequate internet speeds.

causes.cause: Weak Wi-Fi signal to the TV

symptoms: Video quality drops to low resolution
Apps take a long time to load content

fixSteps.title: Test internet speed
fixSteps.instruction: Run a speed test on a phone or computer on the same network. You need at least 5 Mbps for HD and 25 Mbps for 4K.

fixSteps.title: Restart TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both for 60 seconds. Plug in the router first, wait 2 minutes, then plug in the TV.

fixSteps.title: Clear app cache
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Apps > select the buffering app > Clear Cache to remove old data.

fixSteps.title: Use Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the TV directly to the router with an Ethernet cable for maximum bandwidth and stability.

fixSteps.title: Lower streaming quality
fixSteps.instruction: In the streaming app's settings, reduce video quality from 4K/UHD to 1080p or 720p to reduce bandwidth requirements.

whenToCallPro: Buffering issues are almost always network-related. Consider upgrading your internet plan or using an external streaming device for better app performance.

faq.q: Why does my Hisense TV keep buffering?
faq.a: The most common cause is insufficient internet speed or a weak Wi-Fi connection. Use Ethernet or move the router closer to the TV.

faq.q: What internet speed do I need for 4K streaming?
faq.a: A minimum of 25 Mbps is recommended for 4K streaming. For multiple devices streaming simultaneously, you may need 50+ Mbps.

title: Audio and Video Out of Sync
shortDescription: The audio is noticeably delayed or ahead relative to the video.
fullDescription:
Audio delay (lip sync issues) on Hisense TVs occurs when there is a noticeable mismatch between what you see and what you hear. The audio may be behind or ahead of the corresponding video.

This issue is most common when using external audio devices connected through HDMI ARC, Bluetooth, or optical cable. Each connection type introduces different amounts of processing delay. Bluetooth has the most delay, followed by optical, with HDMI ARC typically having the least.

The TV's built-in audio processing features can also introduce latency. Features like virtual surround sound, dialog enhancement, and volume leveling all add processing time to the audio signal.
symptoms: Lip movements do not match words

fixSteps.title: Adjust audio delay setting
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Audio Delay and adjust the slider to sync audio with video. Positive values delay audio, negative values advance it.

fixSteps.title: Switch to HDMI ARC from Bluetooth
fixSteps.instruction: If using a Bluetooth speaker, switch to HDMI ARC connection for significantly lower latency.

fixSteps.title: Disable audio processing
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off features like DTS Virtual:X, Dialog Enhancement, and Volume Leveling from the Sound settings menu.

fixSteps.title: Set audio format to PCM
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Sound > Digital Audio Format and set to PCM. This is processed faster than Dolby Digital or other surround formats.

fixSteps.title: Enable Game Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Picture > Picture Mode > Game. This minimizes all video and audio processing for the lowest possible latency.

whenToCallPro: Audio sync issues are adjustable through settings and rarely require professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense TV audio delay lip sync fix

faq.q: Why is my Hisense TV audio out of sync?
faq.a: Audio delay is most commonly caused by external speakers adding processing time. Use the Audio Delay slider in Sound settings to compensate.

faq.q: Which connection has the least audio delay?
faq.a: HDMI ARC/eARC has the least delay. Optical is slightly more. Bluetooth has the most delay and is not recommended for real-time audio sync.

title: Roku TV Wireless Connection Error
shortDescription: The Hisense Roku TV cannot connect to the selected Wi-Fi network.
fullDescription:
Error 014 on Hisense Roku TVs indicates the TV can see your wireless network but fails to connect to it. This error is specific to Hisense models running the Roku operating system rather than VIDAA.

Hisense manufactures TVs with multiple operating systems including VIDAA, Roku, Google TV, and Fire TV. Roku-based Hisense TVs share the same error codes and troubleshooting steps as other Roku devices.

The 014 error is primarily caused by authentication failures, usually from an incorrect password, changed router settings, or MAC address filtering on the router.

fixSteps.title: Re-enter Wi-Fi password
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Set up connection > Wireless and select your network. Re-enter the password carefully.

fixSteps.title: Restart TV and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both devices for 60 seconds. Start the router first, wait 2 minutes, then plug in the TV.

fixSteps.title: Reset network connection
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > Advanced System Settings > Network Connection Reset to clear all stored network data.

fixSteps.title: Check router settings
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router and verify MAC filtering is disabled. Ensure the security type is WPA2/WPA3 which is compatible with Roku.

fixSteps.title: Try mobile hotspot
fixSteps.instruction: Create a mobile hotspot on your phone and try connecting the TV to it. This helps determine if the issue is with your router specifically.

whenToCallPro: If the TV cannot connect to any network including a mobile hotspot after all troubleshooting, the Wi-Fi hardware may be defective.

faq.q: What does error 014 mean on Hisense Roku TV?
faq.a: Error 014 means the TV found your Wi-Fi network but cannot connect. The most common cause is an incorrect password.

faq.q: Does my Hisense TV use Roku or VIDAA?
faq.a: It depends on the model. R-Series Hisense TVs use Roku, while A-Series and U-Series typically use VIDAA or Google TV. Check the home screen interface.
